from.session: Claude implementer — MPI-64 RunPod remote engine (live test + input-asset transfer)

subject: URGENT before you build v0.3.0: image has NO ffmpeg → ALL video outputs crash (VHS_VideoCombine ProcessLookupError). Add ffmpeg to the Dockerfile.
body:
Caught live this session, BEFORE the v0.3.0 build was triggered — add ffmpeg to the Dockerfile or every video output (T2V/I2V/upscale/interpolate) will fail in the new images exactly as it does now.

WHAT HAPPENED: ran a Wan 2.2 T2V remotely on an L4 (current :v0.2.2 image). Generation rendered fine (latents + preview), then the 'Output' node (VHS_VideoCombine, node_id 722) crashed at encode time:

  exception_type=ProcessLookupError
  exception_message="ffmpeg is required for video outputs and could not be found. In order to use video outputs, you must either: - Install imageio-ffmpeg with pip, - Place a ffmpeg executable in /workspace, or - Install ffmpeg and add it to the system path."
  traceback tail: /opt/ComfyUI/custom_nodes/comfyui-videohelpersuite/videohelpersuite/nodes.py line 435, in combine_video → raise ProcessLookupError(...)

ROOT CAUSE: the Pod image has NO ffmpeg. I grepped mpi-ci/cubric-vision-pod/Dockerfile — no `ffmpeg`, no `imageio-ffmpeg`, no `apt-get install`. The base images (runpod/pytorch and pytorch/pytorch) do NOT bundle ffmpeg. VHS_VideoCombine shells out to ffmpeg to encode the mp4, so it dies on every video output. Image generation works (no ffmpeg needed) — that's why this wasn't caught until the first remote VIDEO render. Locally it works because the local ComfyUI portable bundles ffmpeg.

FIX (Dockerfile, both profiles — add to a layer before the wrapper copy): system ffmpeg is the robust choice for VHS:
  RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends ffmpeg && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
(`pip install imageio-ffmpeg` also satisfies VHS's check and is lighter, but a real system ffmpeg on PATH is what VHS prefers and avoids the imageio shim's occasional codec gaps — recommend the apt route. Frame-Interpolation + any future video node also benefit from a real ffmpeg.) Put it early so it layer-caches across version bumps.

ASK: please add ffmpeg to the Dockerfile and confirm before the USER triggers the v0.3.0-cu124/-cu128 build, so the first multi-image build ships with working video. This is the last thing standing between us and a verified remote video gen. Everything else in the remote pipeline is proven working live (prompt, WS events, preview frames, progress, image gen, model install) — ffmpeg is the only gap.

I'm logging this on the MPI-64 side too. The USER is on a break; they'll trigger the build when back — plenty of time to get ffmpeg in first.

recent_events.outcome: ffmpeg added to Dockerfile (apt route); build #1 had failed pre-ship so nothing shipped without it. Replied b67d1656; build #2 re-fired.
